annotations.label: Hodge star operator

anchor.snippet: the Hodge star operator or Hodge star is a linear map defined on the exterior algebra

annotations.note: No Hodge star operator is defined anywhere in Mathlib4 (no `hodgeStar`/`Hodge` results in `Mathlib/`).

annotations.label: Hodge dual in 3D Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: in an oriented 3-dimensional Euclidean space, an oriented plane can be represented by the exterior product of two basis vectors

annotations.note: Mathlib has `crossProduct` and `Orientation.volumeForm`, but no Hodge-dual interpretation linking bivectors to vectors in 3D.

annotations.label: Induced inner product on k-vectors

anchor.snippet: This induces an inner product on k -vectors

annotations.note: Mathlib has `ExteriorPower` and inner product spaces but no induced inner product on the exterior power of an inner product space.

annotations.label: Unit n-vector

anchor.snippet: The unit n -vector

annotations.note: The dual notion of the unit top-form is captured by `Orientation.volumeForm`, but the unit n-vector itself in the exterior algebra is not defined.

annotations.label: Right complement of a basis element

anchor.snippet: the right complement of a basis element

annotations.note: No notion of right complement on basis elements of an exterior algebra appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hodge star operator (constructive formula)

anchor.snippet: The Hodge star operator is a linear operator on the exterior algebra of V

annotations.note: No Hodge star is defined on the exterior algebra in Mathlib.

annotations.note: No formula for the Hodge star on orthonormal k-vectors in Mathlib.

annotations.note: No left Hodge dual in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hodge star is an isometry

anchor.snippet: the Hodge star operator takes an orthonormal basis to an orthonormal basis

annotations.note: No Hodge star, hence no isometry property in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: In two dimensions with the normalized Euclidean metric and orientation given by the ordering

anchor.snippet: A common example of the Hodge star operator is the case

annotations.label: Hodge star and cross product

anchor.snippet: The Hodge star relates the exterior and cross product in three dimensions

annotations.note: `crossProduct` exists in Mathlib but is not identified with the Hodge dual of an exterior product.

annotations.label: Axial vector–bivector isomorphism

anchor.snippet: the Hodge star provides an isomorphism between axial vectors and bivectors

annotations.note: No formal Hodge-star isomorphism between vectors and bivectors in 3D appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cross product corresponds to commutator

anchor.snippet: cross product of vectors corresponds to the commutator Lie bracket of linear operators

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `crossProduct` and Lie brackets but does not identify the cross product with the commutator of associated linear operators.

annotations.label: Hodge star as involution on 2-forms in 4D Riemannian case

anchor.snippet: If the signature of the metric tensor is all positive, i.e. on a Riemannian manifold , then the Hodge star is an involution

annotations.note: No Hodge star and no associated involution on 2-forms in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Minkowski-signature Hodge star is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Self-dual/anti-self-dual two-forms are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conformal invariance of Hodge star

anchor.snippet: The Hodge star is conformally invariant on n -forms on a 2 n -dimensional vector space

annotations.note: No Hodge star and no statement of conformal invariance on middle-degree forms in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Codifferential
